label: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 2

extraction.one_line_summary: KRON Andy won from a reduced breakaway after race neutralization at 9km-to-go provisional finish due to dangerous rain conditions.
tactical_patterns: Team principals negotiated race neutralization directly with organizers mid-stage, revealing GC teams prioritize safety over early positioning battles.
BIDAL Jose Maria attacked approaching provisional finish despite neutralization agreement, exposing opportunistic willingness to break collective rider consensus.
SOBRERO Matteo prioritized KOM points collection over stage win despite mechanical, demonstrating jersey competition remains active during neutralized stages.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: negative: crashed on Barcelona roundabouts, required team to negotiate peacemaking, signals vulnerability on technical urban circuits
rider_signals.MILESI Lorenzo: negative: abandoned race from red jersey at 18km-to-go, suggests inadequate recovery or injury from previous crash
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: crashed in same conditions that felled multiple riders, confirms struggles with wet technical descending
rider_signals.PICCOLO Andrea: positive: fought back to breakaway after crash then gained race lead, shows exceptional resilience under adverse conditions
rider_signals.KRON Andy: positive: soloed from breakaway after provisional finish showing superior form and opportunism when others assumed neutralization
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: neutral: positioned for sprint among favorites group despite neutralization, maintained alertness when others eased
